Simon Beaufoy
Huge on the screen, A plastic doll stares at us. A commercial for Talky Crissy.
her growing hair.... The screen divides in two to accommodate both Talky Crissy and a squadron of B52s unloading its cargo. Hundreds of bombs unzip the sky.
The screen divides into smaller squares accommodating the best and the worst of the early 1970's. Clips of Hawaii FiveO, Here's Lucy, The Mary Tyler Moore Show, stills from
commercials, documentary footage from Town Bloody Hall, each

taking centre screen for a few seconds and then shrinking to allow the next clip to take precedence. "The Kenwood Chef does everything but cook. That's what wives are for". "Flying Dutchman tobacco: lead Women around by the nose". "Don't worry, darling, you didn't burn the beer. Schlitz Malt". Then a news clip takes centre screen:

Good evening, in a landmark ruling, the Supreme Court today legalized abortions. A majority from Texas and Georgia said that the decision to end a pregnancy during the first three months belongs to a woman and her doctor not the government.
Town Bloody Hall takes precedence as a woman in the audience accuses Norman Mailer on the stage.
...the dog talks, you know! That a woman should talk at all is something you are finding a little hard to take...
BATTLE OF THE SEXES - Simon Work File - 4/7/16
Which fades in favour of the doll Talky Crissy again: But there's someone new in town.
EXT. FOREST HILLS TENNIS COURT. US OPEN. DAY.

BILLIE JEAN KING's 29 year-old face, big on the screen, concentrating hard. We hear a grunt and the noise of a serve. A flash and Billie Jean has reached the ball, returned it. The ball comes back, but she is across the court with lightning speed and putting every ounce of energy into the shot, returns it again. We are inside her head: only the sound of her furious breathing, the ball and the squeak of tennis shoes can be

Back comes the ball, again and again, each time, Billie Jean punches it back until.... ...The world crashes in: a sudden surge of applause and cheering. The focus on Billie Jean's face finally relaxes into a huge smile of triumph. UMPIRE V/O Game, set and match to Miss King. She throws the racket high into the air and we see she is surrounded by thousands of fans applauding wildly. LARRY KING, Billie Jean's husband, jumps onto the court and embraces her. Title: Battle of the Sexes
EXT. HIGH RISE OFFICE. NEW YORK. DAY. A faceless, glass office building. Distant traffic and
honking horns. Moving in on one of the many windows, we find...

...and Billie Jean King's triumph in the US Open adds another Grand Slam to a list of titles that makes her the most successful woman player of all time and brings a phone call from a very special fan....
BATTLE OF THE SEXES - Simon Work File - 4/7/16
INT. OFFICE. NEW YORK. DAY.

...fifty-four year old BOBBY RIGGS, tennis racket in hand, staring at the TV. On the TV, Billie Jean is on court holding up the trophy from the US Open. The TV cuts to a boardroom where she is now talking on a speaker-phone to PRESIDENT NIXON.
Hello, Mister President.
I just wanted to congratulate you on your great successes this year and being the first sportswoman to earn over a hundred thousand dollars in a season.
Thank you, Mister President. Thank you very much, Sir.
With five Wimbledon titles, a French Open win and now a fourth US Open crown, the Old Lady as she is affectionately known on the circuitshows no sign of hanging up her tennis shoes any time soon....

Bobby switches off the TV with a sigh. He bats a ball of scrunched up paper expertly into the trash, wanders back behind a large, largely empty desk. He sets the Newton's Cradle desk toy going, his eyes following it's progress back and forth for a while. Snaps out of it. Presses the intercom.
Has that contract from Diemberger come in yet, Mike?
Oh, yes, Sir. I turned it around yesterday. You did?

Bobby switches off the TV with a sigh. He bats a ball of scrunched up paper expertly into the trash, wanders back behind a large, largely empty desk. He sets the Newton's Cradle desk toy going, his eyes following it's progress back and forth for a while. He's looking at Bobby. Bobby points at himself. Really? Me? He stands up. Sighs. Here we go. Snaps out of it. Presses the intercom.
Has that contract from Diemberger come in yet, Mike?
Oh, yes, Sir. I turned it around yesterday. You did?
BATTLE OF THE SEXES - Simon Work File - 4/7/16
My name is Bobby and I am an addict. Hi, Bobby.
At least that's what Priscilla says. My wife. Lovely lady.
His face briefly shines and then he remembers.
She's gonna leave me unless I quit gambling. A couple of 'we hear you, Bobby's from the room.
Puzzles me, this word: gambling. Every time she gets the car out the
garage Priscilla's gambling big

My point? Everybody gambles all the time. Life's a gamble, right? That's the thrill of it: this way or that way, the yes or the no, the heads or the tails of being alive! Yet here I am and here you are, heads bowed, ashamed of ourselves. But that ain't right. You fellas aren't here cos you're gamblerswe're all gamblers. You're here cos you're terrible gamblers. That's what the problem is. You lose, right? And you end up here. More ashamed nods from the Gamblers.
I get it. But there's one thing worse than losing, friends: it's not being in the game. If you're not in game, what's the point?
We're dead men walking, right? I mean, look at us!
This is taking a surprising turn. Even Bobby is cheering up.
Heads up, fellas, we can turn this around, we can get back in the game, we will get back in the game... Everyone's listening now. This isn't the inspirational speech
they were expecting, but they like it.
Yeah, a thing! An edge, an angle, the inside track, the sniff in the wind, the thing that turns you from a gambler to a hustler, from a loser to a winner. A thing!
This is not what we should be talking about here.
Why do you want to stop us doing the one thing we really love?
The Leader is nonplussed.
Sir, all they gotta do is learn to do it better. Now, who's dealing?
He throws a pack of cards on the table, rolls up his sleeves.
Isn't this fun?
BATTLE OF THE SEXES - Simon Work File - 4/7/16 INT. NEW YORK HOTEL ROOM -- BEDROOM/BATHROOM. EVENING.

A silver trophy on the bedside table, surrounded by dozens of FLOWER ARRANGEMENTS. Billie Jean's husband LARRY KING (28, tall, immaculately dressed) reads the cards that came with the flowers. Billie Jean is out of sight in the bathroom.

Flowers from your parents! We should have them over when we get back to California. A home-cooked meal, wouldn't that be something? Billie Jean? In the bathroom, in a long evening gown, Billie Jean is
putting on earrings. She is lost in her own thoughts.
We should go. Car's here. Billie?
He goes into the bathroom. Billie-Jean is just staring. Billie.
Can we not go?
Not go? You're the champ, honey. You're guest of honor!
Then he sees that she's serious.
What's wrong?
It isn't working, Larry.
What isn't?
You and me. Us. Being married. We're never in the same place at the same time and when we are, we don't make each other happy.
I'm happy. And if there's more I can do to make you happy, tell me.
It's not you, Larry. You know it's not.
Larry comes up to her and takes the necklace from her. Starts to put it on. BATTLE OF THE SEXES - Simon Work File - 4/7/16
We've had our moments- which marriage doesn't- but we've got through them before and we'll get through them again. The necklace is tied.
There. You look beautiful. A bit tired maybe which is hardly a surprise. Stop winning everything, honey.
A weary smile from Billie.
Tomorrow you're staying in bed and I'm doing everything, okay?
Thank you. You're a good man, Larry King.
He offers her his hand.
Come on. They can't start the dancing without you.
BATTLE OF THE SEXES - Simon Work File - 4/7/16 INT. FOREST HILLS CLUB -- BALL ROOM. EVENING. The Tournament Ball. Larry and Billie Jean alone on the dance

floor, waltzing elegantly- the perfect, happy couple. They are surrounded by hundreds of people in evening dress, all of whom applaud as the song comes to an end. Larry kisses Billie Jean and welcomes the rest of the party-goers to join them on the floor. GLADYS HELDMAN, a feisty forty-year old marches onto the floor, interrupting Billie and Larry.
Have you seen this??
Hey, Gladys.
BATTLE OF THE SEXES - Simon Work File - 4/7/16
You want a dance?
I want a gun. They've just snuck out this press release, the lowlifes.
Gladys waves a piece of paper in front of Billie. Billie grabs it. Reads.
Which low-lifes?
Our so-called friends, the United States Lawn Tennis Association.
Gladys jabs a finger at one particular line. Billie Jean's eyes widen.
What?! This is outrageous.
It's got Jack Kramer's fingerprints all over it.
Absolutely right. And I know where he's hiding. Come on, Gladys.
She grabs Gladys and they march off the dance floor.
Good luck!
INT. FOREST HILL COUNTRY CLUB -- DINING ROOM. NIGHT.
Gladys and Billie Jean walk between the tables.
It's a slap in the face, that's what this is.
Slap on the wrist, more like. How dare we girls do so well?! Better put us in our place!
We can't let them get away with it.
BATTLE OF THE SEXES - Simon Work File - 4/7/16 INT. FOREST HILLS CLUB -- LIBRARY. EVENING.
JACK KRAMER and BOB SANDERS are sitting in club chairs, relaxing. DANA, a young waitress, hands them whiskies.
Thank you, honey.
Sure, Mister Kramer.
He follows the rear view with his eyes as she goes. Gladys and Billie Jean come marching in.
Surprised in their lair.
An unexpected pleasure, ladies. You both look sensational, if I may say.
You may not.
You're not supposed to be in here, you know-GLADYS - because I'm a woman or a Jew?
Oh, we have a Jewish member now. Don't we, Bob?
One black member as well. Progress!
But no women.
Not as yet, no.
Jack pats a seat for Gladys, oblivious to the daggers shot his way.
What can we do for you ladies? Glass of something?
BATTLE OF THE SEXES - Simon Work File - 4/7/16
Gladys sits, gets out a cigarette. Bob leans forward to light it. She pointedly lights it herself.
I always find it's best to be clear before committing cold-blooded murder, so, to clarify: the Pacific Southwest Tournament press release.
She bangs the offending piece of paper down on the coffee table, rattling the china.
It says you're offering twelve thousand dollars to the men's winner and one thousand five hundred dollars to the women's.
That's either a misprint or a provocation. I might get off with manslaughter.
No, those are the terms.
The men's prize has to be that high to attract the best players. We're trying to make this the most prestigious tournament in America.
And paying the women players less than ever makes it more prestigious. Aha. I get it. I see the logic. Hallelujah, my eyes have been opened.
Said quietly but firmly. Everybody takes notice.
You're lucky she's here. She's like the bomb disposal squadBILLIE JEAN (firmer) - Gladys.
A huff from Gladys. Silence.
BATTLE OF THE SEXES - Simon Work File - 4/7/16
Can I just bring it back around to the money, Jack?
You know I'm sympathetic to your cause, Billie Jean. We've talked about this-BILLIE JEAN We have. Many times. You promised this year would be different.
It's simply a question of what we can afford.
People come to see the men play. They're the draw.
Eight times more of a draw? Sorry?
You're offering the men's winner exactly eight times more than the women's winner. Do we bring in an eighth of the crowd?
I don't know the percentages-BILLIE JEAN They sold the exact same number of tickets today for the women's final as the men's final. Isn't that right, Jack?
I suppose so.
Same sales, same prize money. Seems fair to me.
Be reasonable, there's no way we can afford that.
BATTLE OF THE SEXES - Simon Work File - 4/7/16
Then what's your argument? Just give me a logical argument why you should pay a woman an eighth of what you pay a man and I promise I'll stop asking.
Well, for one thing, the men have to support their families.
I'm the main bread winner in my family.
Look. The men are simply more exciting to watch. They're faster--fact-stronger-fact-
JACK KRAMER BOB SANDERS
-more competitiveBOB SANDERS -just a fact.
It's not your fault. It's biology.
That's the best argument you've got? Really?
It's just the way it is.
We'll boycott the tournament. Fact.
We'd sure miss your pretty faces, but...go right ahead.
BATTLE OF THE SEXES - Simon Work File - 4/7/16
Not only will we boycott, we'll set up our own tournament.
A flicker of panic from Gladys.
At the exact same time as the Pacific Southwest. Um. Fact.
Jack Kramer and Bob Sanders exchange a look. stands. Jack smiles and
Well, be sure and send me tickets. You have a good night, ladies.
He holds open the library door for them.
EXT. FOREST HILLS CLUB -- CORRIDOR. EVENING.
The camera follows Billie Jean and Gladys as they go.
Are we really going to do this?
Sure we're going to do this.
How are we going to do this? No idea.
INT. BOBBY'S HOUSE. EVENING.
An air of tension in this upscale dining room. Bobby, PRISCILLA his second wife and BOBBY'S SON are sitting at the table.
BATTLE OF THE SEXES - Simon Work File - 4/7/16
Run off my feet. Not a minute to think...Whatever it is I'm supposed to be doing there, Priscilla, I'm not doing it.
Daddy says you're an integral part of the company.
Integral. Like the walls? The walkin closet?
It's steady. It's what you need right now.
I know, I know.
Did you go to- your meeting?
Only darned meeting I had. I might have to find another group.
But you went. Oh yeah.
Well done, honey.
She smiles supportively at him. He attempts a smile back. Another silence. Bobby's son peppers his food with a outsize pepper grinder. Appraises it.
(sly) I wonder how much pepper corns are in here?
It's not how much, it's how many. And stop wondering. Or you can go wonder in your room.
Mom...I was just asking Dad an educational question, right?
BATTLE OF THE SEXES - Simon Work File - 4/7/16
Oh, I...I dunno, Junior.
I'd say 'bout a thousand.
(cracking) No way, there's at least five thousand in there.
I'd put a dollar on a thousandPRISCILLA - young man!
Bobby's son gives the pepper grinder another provocative twist, winks at his Dad and mouths "one dollar".
Absolutely not, young man. We do not gamble at the table.
We do not gamble any place, Bobby.
Exactly. Any place.
Bobby pats his mouth with his napkin. Dabs at the sweat on his brow. Gets up.
You know, honey, now I remember, there is something I need to do at the office. Now?
Your Daddy put his faith in me. Don't want to let him down.
She smiles up at him uncertainly- benefit of the doubt.
Well, okay. Proud of you, honey.
EXT. TOWN TENNIS CENTER -- COURT. MANHATTAN. EVENING.
Men's laughter, drinks, music. Wedges of greenbacks everywhere. In the middle of the table, surrounded by dollars, a set of car keys.
BATTLE OF THE SEXES - Simon Work File - 4/7/16

Bizarrely, two Alsatians are bounding around a tennis court, Bobby on the end of their lead, being towed after them. On the other side of the net is JACK, retired business tycoon and amateur tennis player. Equally bizarre is the location of the court; on the flat roof of a skyscraper near the belching smoke stacks of the
Con Edison Power Plant.

Loving the show are the Boys: HANK, ex-baseball pro: retired KEN and gentle HERB, a tennis star in the 50's, now in a wheelchair and LORNIE KUHLE, younger friend of Bobby's and tennis pro. Somehow, Bobby manages to reach the ball and get it back over the net. The Boys are loving it, the dogs are loving it and most of all, so is Bobby.
Dammit, Bobby....
Teach them to serve and we could get them on the Circuit! Good boys, good boys go, go... He wins the point.
A chair. C'mon, guys...
Two chairs and I'll raise you both
Done. Take him, Jack!

Lornie rushes two chairs onto Bobby's side of the court that he now has to dodge around with the dogs. Chaos. Bobby is leaping over the chairs, getting the lead tangled in the legs...and yet somehow, the ball keeps being returned. More dollars are put down on the table by the Boys. There's big money on this game. A fierce exchange of shots, culminating in a lot of barking and a massive lob from Bobby. Everyone watches its progress high into the sky and then down on the baseline. Right on the baseline. Jack scrabbles back for it. Doesn't get there. Bobby leaps the net, shakes his hand.
Good game, Jack.
BATTLE OF THE SEXES - Simon Work File - 4/7/16
Dammit, I thought I had you this time, Bobby, I really did.
Dejected, he walks off. The boys watch him go.
He keeps betting me. What do I do?
Thing about Jack is- he can afford to lose.
Jack gets the car keys from the bowl in the middle of the table. Throws them to Bobby. Towels his face down.
What's Priscilla going to say, Bobby? That's what you gotta ask yourself.
Don't spoil it, Jack, you know?
Priscilla? You still married, Bobby?
He sure will be if he turns up in a Rolls Royce.
He sure won't be if he turns up in a Rolls Royce.
Whadd'ya mean?
You think Priscilla won't know where it came from? Even Bobby's gonna find it hard talking that one away. "Hi, honey, this guy on the corner just gave me a Silver Shadow..." Think that'll cut it?
What's to talk away? You've just won a damned Rolls Royce.
Herb, Bobby doesn't gamble anymore. You don't?
BATTLE OF THE SEXES - Simon Work File - 4/7/16
No! I'm Gamblers Anonymous once a week, shrink twice a week. Bobby Riggs is as clean as Montana snow. You are?
Absolutely. A reformed character. That's what my psychoanalyst's report says and Priscilla's very happy about that, I can tell you.
How did you ever get the shrink to write that?
The chump keeps losing at Black Jack. But Lornie's got a point. It's a big car to hide in a drawer. No hurry, Jack.
Bobby throws the keys back.
You saying I'm not good for a bet?
No, I'm just saying keep the engine warm, don't scratch my paintwork, you know?
The Boys laugh as Jack curses and walks away.
INT. TOWN TENNIS CENTER -- LOCKER ROOM. MANHATTAN. LATER. 14 Teak lockers, steam coming from the showers, a man on a
massage table getting treatment and a card table. Lots of men in towels lounging around. Hank is reading an article about Billie Jean. Bobby is getting changed. Notices the article.
"The hundred thousand dollar girl". Don't see anybody on the Senior Tour getting a hundred gees.
BATTLE OF THE SEXES - Simon Work File - 4/7/16
Reckons she should get more. Same as the men.
She's not happy with a hundred thousand dollars, now? That Billie Jean King. Just what is her problem?
It's all equality this, equality that now, Bobby. Bra burning and the like.
Why would they pay women the same as the guys? Why would they?
I say there's nothing wrong with watching a nice girl in a short
skirt running after a ball.

No argument there, Jack, but I could beat any woman on the planet. Any of them. And what are they paying me on the Seniors? Anybody offering me equal prize money? No, sir. Peanuts, that's what I'm getting. Peanuts.
Silence. Then:
That would be a bet. What?
Bobby Riggs versus Billie Jean King. I'd pay a lot to see that. All the men stare at Bobby, suddenly interested.
INT. GENE SHAWCOVE'S HAIR SALON. DAY.

Nine tennis player renegades are having their hair done: Billie Jean King, ROSIE CASALS (El Salvadorian-American), JULIE HELDMAN (Gladys's daughter), VALERIE ZIEGENFUSS (very blonde), PEACHES BARTKOWICZ (Polish-American), NANCY RICHEY (always knitting), KRISTY PIGEON (only 20), KERRY MELVILLE REID (English) and JUDY TEGART DALTON (older, Australian).
BATTLE OF THE SEXES - Simon Work File - 4/7/16
Gladys smokes as she pays the salon with a personal check. Billie Jean can't sit still in the chair where she's meant to be having her hair done by MARILYN BARNETT (late twenties).
Gladys? What if some of the men decide they want to join our tour? You know, in solidarity?
Billie Jean pops out of her seat. Marilyn's hands reach after her, helpless to keep her in the chair.
No! That's the whole point! We have to show everyone we can bring in the crowds without the men.
How are ticket sales going?
Good! Fine! We're working on it!
Are we going to have our hair done before every event? Because I could get used to this.
Some of the women make noises of agreement.
Right! Um. Gladys?
Billie Jean sits back in her chair, ceding the floor to Gladys. Marilyn resumes styling Billie Jean's hair.
The salon is a once-only, because the press are here and because I love you. Tomorrow, the press aren't here and I don't love you.
What about me, Mom? You'll still love me, right?
Some of the girls laugh.
This is a budget tour, ladies. And yes, you'll be sharing rooms, so you might as well start getting friendly.
I call bunking with Valerie!
BATTLE OF THE SEXES - Simon Work File - 4/7/16
I thought you were getting us a sponsor, Gladys.
I was, I am and I will. Until then, we're broke, so shut up.
Wait, what about our prize money?
Why are you worried, Val? You only get prize money if you win.
Lots of laughter and mock 'oohs' from the girls. Billie Jean pops back out of her chair. Marilyn frowns.
Listen, everyone. I know how much you're risking here, because I'm risking the exact same thing. More.
No. The same. We're all putting our careers on the line for this. So I thank you. For your bravery. And your balls.
The women applaud and whoop. Billie Jean grins.
All right! You've got thirty minutes until we go face the press! How long? Outrage from the girls.
Okay, twenty.
As Gladys passes by Billie, Billie grabs her arm.
Anything from the USLTA? From Jack?
Not a word. They're up to something.
Gladys walks away. HANDS clasp Billie Jean's shoulders. Billie Jean looks up at Marilyn, who holds onto her.
BATTLE OF THE SEXES - Simon Work File - 4/7/16
Sit. Please.
Sorry. I'm excited. Or nervous. Something.
Billie Jean sits in front of Marilyn. BONNY, the stylist working on Julie, pipes up:
Sounds like you girls are getting men to pay attention to what you're saying for once.
You know what would really get their attention? A sex strike.
Wouldn't that punish us as much as the men?
I don't even think I'd miss it. My ex went about it like he was fixing the carburetor on the Oldsmobile.
And he never could get that damned car to start.
The girls laugh.
(Quiet, to Billie Jean) At least women know where the carburetor is. Right?
Billie Jean doesn't know how to answer. Marilyn leans over so her face is level with Billie's, intimate:
So, Billie Jean. What do you want? ...What?
With your hair. What do you want to do with it?
Oh, um. It doesn't matter. Just get it out of my face.
BATTLE OF THE SEXES - Simon Work File - 4/7/16
You don't care what you look like? Someone as pretty as you?
What? No. I'm not pretty. I mean thank you. But that's not me.
If you say so.
Billie Jean can't look away from this woman's direct gaze.
What's your name again?
Marilyn Barnett. You're all tennis players?
Yeah. You don't watch tennis?
I don't own a TV.
Marilyn runs her fingers through her hair. It feels good. Billie Jean closes her eyes. Her face relaxes. She opens her eyes, feeling caught.
Sorry. That feels nice.
We're close on her face, Marilyn's hands. It's sensual.
What is that? Is that perfume?
I like to put lavender oil on my wrists. To relax the customer.
Marilyn leans over so her face is next to Billie Jean's.
Why don't you let me give you a trim? You'll come out feeling like a new person.
I don't think a haircut has ever made me feel anything like that.
BATTLE OF THE SEXES - Simon Work File - 4/7/16
Well, you've never had me cut your hair. Relax. I promise I'll make you happy.
Marilyn goes to work on Billie Jean's hair. Billie Jean watches her in the mirror, transfixed in spite of herself.
INT. FRONT DESK. GENE SHAWCOVE'S HAIR SALON. DAY.
The Nine are hurrying out the door. Billy Jean lingers, putting on her jacket. Her hair is shorter and much more stylish. She watches Marilyn sweep up.
Thanks again. I really like what you did with it.
It was my pleasure. Good luck.
Rosie bangs on the window. They're late. Coming!
BILLIE JEAN
Billie Jean roots around in her bag, pulls out some fliers. She hands them out to various HAIR DRESSERS in the salon.
Here. We're playing in San Diego next month.
Billie Jean hands a flier to Marilyn. Marilyn smiles. Billie Jean looks away, nervous.
Okay. I gotta run. Bye.
And Billie Jean is out the door. Marilyn watches her go. Bonny comes over, fanning herself with the flier.
You think she knows she's gay?
Dunno. But she sure she knows I am.
Billie Jean peers back over her shoulder. Eye contact. Marilyn waves. Billie Jean quickly turns away. BATTLE OF THE SEXES - Simon Work File - 4/7/16
INT. CONFERENCE ROOM. DAY.
A room full of Photographers and Press, all facing the newlycoiffed Nine. They are led out onto the stage by Gladys who does a lot of noisy choreographing.
INT. CONFERENCE ROOM. STAGE. DAY.
Peaches, there. Kerry, Kerry, I know he's cute, but can you justthank you. Judy, here.
Larry King is sitting at the back, surrounded by paperwork. He gets up and goes to Billie Jean, murmurs in her ear.
Guess who showed up?
He nods. There at the back is Jack Kramer. Billie moves out of the line-up and walks through the Press. Jack.
Billie Jean, what you're doing here, this is a big mistake.
Are you here to give us another option, Jack?
On behalf of the USLTA, I'm here to ask you girls not to do anything hasty.
So what are you offering? Equal prize money? Women at every tournament?
I'm offering to keep you in the United States Lawn Tennis Association.
